steps.title: Calculate the mean of data set A
steps.content:
Data set A contains: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. To find the mean, sum all values and divide by the count:
$$\text{Sum} = 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6 + 7 = 28$$
$$\text{Mean of A} = \frac{28}{7} = 4$$

steps.title: Calculate the mean of data set B
steps.content:
Data set B contains: 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 4. Sum all values and divide by the count:
$$\text{Sum} = 1 + 1 + 2 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 4 = 16$$
$$\text{Mean of B} = \frac{16}{7} \approx 2.29$$

steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content: Mean of A = 4 and Mean of B = $\frac{16}{7} \approx 2.29$. Since $4 > 2.29$, the mean of data set A is greater than the mean of data set B. That matches choice D — D is correct.
